questions.question: Highlight a sentence or phrase from the text that supports your answer to Part A.

responses.content: She feels like she’s gonna cry
She puts it far away from the sweater

questions.question: Part A: Which of the following abilities does the narrator mainly believe older people have that younger people don't?

responses.content: She cries

questions.question: Part A: What is the main conflict Rachel faces in the story?

responses.content: D. She is forced to wear a sweater that isn't hers and can't effectively stand up for herself.

questions.question: Part B: Which evidence from the text BEST shows how Rachel responds to this conflict?

responses.content: A. “That's when everything I've been holding in since this morning, since when Mrs. Price put the sweater on my desk, finally lets go, and all of a sudden I'm crying in front of everybody.”

questions.question: Which of the following inferences about Deza's character is best supported by the text?

responses.content: Because Mrs. Scott thought Deza was cheating.

questions.question: Which statement best expresses the central idea of this excerpt from “The Mighty Miss Malone”?

responses.content: A. Education offers hope even during the hardest times.

questions.question: Which detail from the text best supports the answer to the previous question?

responses.content: D. “Early every morning, Mother and I would leave the camp and walk for half a hour to downtown Flint. Jimmie would go his own way.”

questions.question: Which statement best describes Deza's thoughts about her Flint teachers?

responses.content: She thinks being back in school is great
Because shes smart

questions.question: Re-read paragraphs 36-41. What does Deza's reaction to Loretta reveal about her character?

responses.content: She flipped the paper back and flipped it again and she banged it because she thought it would make her grade get higher
She says she's gonna do better next time and make sure her grade is better

questions.question: What do Brian's expectations of rescue reveal about how he views his current setting?

responses.content: In small bushplanes
Pick him up and take him home
A bushplanes with floats on the lake

questions.question: Reread paragraphs 7-9. Why does Brian evaluate his surroundings so carefully?

responses.content: That he's hungry
They will take him home
They will land on the lake with small bushplanes floats

questions.question: How does the setting in paragraphs 12-14 cause Brian to change from feeling hopeless to taking action?

responses.content: Because he's hungry and the lake makes him more hungrier
A juicy hamburger with tomatoes and double fries with ketchup and a thick chocolate shake.
Something to eat

questions.question: This part of the story takes place in the wilderness. What challenge does this setting create for Brian?

responses.content: C. The setting's lack of food sources forces Brian to confront the reality of his survival needs

questions.question: Which detail from the text best supports the answer to the previous question?

responses.content: D. “He reached into his pockets and took out everything he had”

questions.question: What does the word agenda mean as it is used in paragraph 22?

responses.content: Mom and daughter

Her mom said on paragraph 4 “Oh sweetie, you startled me. How was it?” And on paragraph 8 and 9 it says “Mrs. Winterbottom suddenly leaned over and kissed Phoebe's cheek. "I'm not a baby, you know," Phoebe said, wiping off the kiss.” And on paragraph 15 she says “ Phoebe shouted at her mother.”
They get mad at her and shot at her complaining about cheerleading.

questions.question: What message does Phoebe find on the steps?

responses.content: Agenda means having her own plans.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the reactions of Phoebe and Prudence to the note that arrives at their door?

responses.content: She finds a cheerleading try out in the envelope.
“In the course of a lifetime, what does it matter?“

questions.question: How does the arrival of the note affect Sal's feelings?

responses.content: She feels sad and ignored almost like she’s invisible. I know because it says  “ She was invisible to Prudence and Phoebe, though. They did not notice.”

questions.question: Which of the following best states a theme of the excerpt?

responses.content: C. The mysterious messenger is attempting to divide the Winterbottoms even further

questions.question: Which sentence from the text most strongly supports your answer to the previous question?

responses.content: B. “She had been asking me why I had not said anything to my father about Mrs. Cadaver and Mr. Birkway, and I told her that I was waiting for the right time.”

activities.text_title: Roll of Thunder, Hear My Cry

questions.question: Based on the context, what does the word “amenities” mean?

responses.content: Because they don’t like other colored people because they are racist.
He said it’s gonna stop the shopping in Vicksburg.
He’s losing customers and shoppers.

questions.question: Why does Papa tell Stacey he is “born blessed”?

responses.content: Paying extra or doing more for something that isn’t necessary.
After all the nonsense
He had a trip to go to but didn’t go with Mr. Morrison.

questions.question: Why is Papa in a better position than Mr. Avery and Mr. Lanier to stand up to Thurston Wallace?

responses.content: Because he’s special.
He names his own land

questions.question: How does Papa feel about Mr. Avery and Mr. Lanier's decision to stop shopping in Vicksburg?

responses.content: Because he’s getting getting paid.
Because if he does he could get in trouble by the people he’s working for.
Because if they do resist they would end up with no place for them to go and they would get kicked off the land plot they have.

questions.question: Highlight a sentence from the text that shows Papa's feelings toward Mr. Avery and Mr. Lanier's decision to stop shopping in Vicksburg.

responses.content: He feels somewhat mad.
He feels mad because then they won’t have enough to pay the taxes and the house.
That they have to do what they need to do because they risked going to shop in Vicksburg.

questions.question: What is the central conflict in this story?

responses.content: A. Papa must choose between his family responsibilities and his railroad job

questions.question: Which sentence from the text best supports the answer to the previous question?

responses.content: D. “I pray to God there was a way we could stay in this thing, but we can't go on no chain gang, David.”

questions.question: In lines 4 and 5, the speaker says that ”we become strangers in our own home.” Who are the strangers in this home?

responses.content: The parents is telling us that when u hit around the teenager age ur gonna start to become ignorant toward ur parent and lock urself in ur room for a while.

questions.question: Re-read lines 6-8. Which of the following inferences is best supported by these lines?

responses.content: The parent and the teenager.
The parent is saying the line and “we” means the parent and the teenager become strangers.

questions.question: Re-read lines 9-14. These lines primarily suggest that _____.

responses.content: Speaking with words that the parent wouldn’t understand.

questions.question: Write two or three sentences that explain what happens to the “strangers” when “years later, the door opens”?

responses.content: When the door opens years later, the “strangers,”who are the children have grown up and they look unfamiliar with stretched skin and they move past the mom..
